subject: Add Molecule tests for 'dns' role
commit_message:
Add Molecule tests for 'dns' role

This commit supplements 'Molecule test for
infrastructure playbook' with role level tests.

Change-Id: I14cdeedf1c80c0ee06bacf0a391ee1d7ccd6df55
Issue-ID: OOM-1841
Signed-off-by: Bartek Grzybowski <b.grzybowski@partner.samsung.com>

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

1)in "ansible/test/roles/prepare-dns/tasks/main.yml" after task: "Download and archive dnsmasq docker image for the dns role to use" there should be a task that removes docker image from local repository. as we expect image to be loaded in "ansible/roles/dns/tasks/main.yml" step. 
2) do you know why in "ansible/roles/dns/tasks/main.yml" in task "Create simulated hostnames file" we are notifying "Restart dns server container" seams to be too early and in fact one could  expect error as this container is loaded in the next step (I know why it does not fail). Do we even need copy_tar/load image?
3) Have you investigated why idempotence is failing, if yes could you share outcomes? acording to docker_container module description we could expect that this step should do nothing as there is no change in container configuration
4) when failing test does not remove dns container from the host - even if Jenkins slaves are ephemeral it can impact other molecule tests that will be running.

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1
> 
> 1)in "ansible/test/roles/prepare-dns/tasks/main.yml" after task: "Download and archive dnsmasq docker image for the dns role to use" there should be a task that removes docker image from local repository. as we expect image to be loaded in "ansible/roles/dns/tasks/main.yml" step. 
> 2) do you know why in "ansible/roles/dns/tasks/main.yml" in task "Create simulated hostnames file" we are notifying "Restart dns server container" seams to be too early and in fact one could  expect error as this container is loaded in the next step (I know why it does not fail). Do we even need copy_tar/load image?
> 3) Have you investigated why idempotence is failing, if yes could you share outcomes? acording to docker_container module description we could expect that this step should do nothing as there is no change in container configuration
> 4) when failing test does not remove dns container from the host - even if Jenkins slaves are ephemeral it can impact other molecule tests that will be running.

1) It's unrelated to this review and i'm not sure it's necessary, raise a Jira issue on that or propose your own commit.
2) Handlers get executed at the end of the run, that's how Ansible works.
3) This was fixed in > Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1
> 
> 1)in "ansible/test/roles/prepare-dns/tasks/main.yml" after task: "Download and archive dnsmasq docker image for the dns role to use" there should be a task that removes docker image from local repository. as we expect image to be loaded in "ansible/roles/dns/tasks/main.yml" step. 
> 2) do you know why in "ansible/roles/dns/tasks/main.yml" in task "Create simulated hostnames file" we are notifying "Restart dns server container" seams to be too early and in fact one could  expect error as this container is loaded in the next step (I know why it does not fail). Do we even need copy_tar/load image?
> 3) Have you investigated why idempotence is failing, if yes could you share outcomes? acording to docker_container module description we could expect that this step should do nothing as there is no change in container configuration
> 4) when failing test does not remove dns container from the host - even if Jenkins slaves are ephemeral it can impact other molecule tests that will be running.

1) It's unrelated to this commit and i'm not sure it's necessary, please raise a Jira issue on that or propose your own commit to fix it eventually.
2) Handlers get executed at the end of the run, not at declaration place, that's how Ansible works.
3) This was fixed in 87422 but wasn't rebased; what it fails on now is already being fixed in OOM-1852.
4) No it can't, please look at molecules 'test' matrix, 'destroy' is being called before 'create'.

Patch Set 4:

1)you are right
2)thanks for clarification
3)ok, waiting for rebase
4)destroy? it is not about molecule container but dns container and probably it will be like this for every role that spawns own docker. Anyway failure is a failure, until this will not become a visible problem we can live with it
